SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description1Txt: Thriive Cambodia provides capital for production equipment and technical assistants to small businessess, helping them to expand and create new jobs. Loans are repaid through business donation of an equivalent value of in-kind products, services, or job training to impoverished community members.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description2Txt: Thriive Nicaragua provides capital for production equipment and technical assistants to small businessess, helping them to expand and create new jobs. Loans are repaid through business donation of an equivalent value of in-kind products, services, or job training to impoverished community members.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description3Txt: Thriive Guatemala provides capital for production equipment and technical assistants to small businessess, helping them to expand and create new jobs. Loans are repaid through business donation of an equivalent value of in-kind products, services, or job training to impoverished community members.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description4Txt: Thriive Vietnam-Hanoi provides capital for production equipment and technical assistants to small businessess, helping them to expand and create new jobs. Loans are repaid through business donation of an equivalent value of in-kind products, services, or job training to impoverished community members.

SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description1Txt: Whole Forest PRI supports sustainable forestry company with expansion into hardwood business line. The company reduces poverty and incentivizes rainforest conservation through its business model in Ecuador.
SumOfProgramRelatedInvstGrp.Description2Txt: TEMA Nicaragua PRI supports company to produce wheelchairs for those in need in local region.

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To support loan guarantee pool for grassroots businesses

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Climate Justice Alliance via Movement Strategy Center

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: To train women to become business trainers for women living in poverty to become entrepreneurs
Empower victims of domestic violence by giving them training and employment to enable them to financially support themselves and their families

NonCashPropertyContributionGrp.NoncashPropertyDesc: Securities from Transfer of Charitable Remainder Trust

ActyNotPreviouslyRptExpln.ExplanationTxt: SK2 Fund, Inc. began making small grants to US based organizations in 2020. This is a new activity that SK2 Fund, Inc. will continue to carry out in alignment with its charitable mission. At its inception, SK2 Fund, Inc. had included grant-making to foreign organizations and direct programming as activities on its 1023 application. Total grants to US organizations in 2020 included 30,018. Grant recipients are included on Page 11 Part XV.

GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Expenditure Responsibility Statement Part VII-B Line 5C Beneficial Returns - grant purpose to suppor
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: Expenditure Responsibility Statement Part VIIB Line 5C Beneficial Returns grant purpose to support loan guarantee pool for grassroots business pregrant inquiry 11/2019. Grant Amount 17159 Date of Grant 12/11/20 Amount Expended 17159 Final Report No funds diverted Hopebox grant purposeEmpower victims of domestic violence by giving them training and employment to enable them to financially support themselves and their families pregrant inquiry 11/2020. Grant Amount 5000 Date of Grant 12/17/20 Final report pending. No funds diverted. Expenditure Responsibility Statement Part VIIB Line 5C TEMA Nicaragua Date of PRI 6/4/20 Purpose support the production of wheelchairs for those in need in local region PRI Amount 15000 Pregrant inquiry 2020 no funds diverted Imara Tech Date of PRI 5/15/20 Purpose Support company with RD to manufacture and market efficient agriprocessing machinery in Tanzania saving smallholder farmers time and cost. Pregrant inquiry 2020 annual report 2021 no funds diverted. PRI Amount 10000 Whole Forest Date of PRI 6/5/20 Purpose Support sustainable forestry company with expansion into hardwood business line Reduce poverty conserve rainforest. PRI Amount 25000 Pregrant inquiry 2020 Annual Report 2021 No funds diverted. Part VIIA Line 2 SK2 Fund Inc. began making grants and loans to organizations. SK2s mission is to accelerate transition to a more equitable and sustainable world. By making grants and loans to organizations focused on reducing inequality and/or supporting environmental sustainability the foundation increases its ability to carry out its exempt purpose. The foundation maintains written contracts with grantees and recipients of PRI loans. In cases where grant amounts are very small and domestic the foundation will rely on a donor receipt letter. The foundation completes expenditure responsibility for disbursements to forprofits. Form 1023 from 2010) indicated charitable disbursements to foreign organizations but not domestic.
